behaviors.behavior: readChunk reads only [fromByte, EOF) from disk
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-io.test.ts > issues a positional read of only (size - fromByte) bytes - PASS; a real FileHandle wrapper records 100,001 bytes requested against a 1,000,001-byte file

behaviors.behavior: fromByte 0 still yields the whole file (inbox-derive caller)
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-io.test.ts > fromByte 0 returns the whole file - PASS

behaviors.behavior: A mid-file read returns exactly the tail
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-io.test.ts > returns exactly [fromByte, EOF) - PASS

behaviors.behavior: fromByte past EOF returns empty with the correct size
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-io.test.ts > clamps a fromByte past EOF to an empty read - PASS

behaviors.behavior: Negative fromByte clamps to 0
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-io.test.ts > clamps a negative fromByte to 0 - PASS

behaviors.behavior: Empty file is handled
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-io.test.ts > handles an empty file - PASS

behaviors.behavior: No read is issued when fromByte is already at EOF
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-io.test.ts > does not read at all when fromByte is already at EOF - PASS (reads.length == 0)

behaviors.behavior: A short read is completed by the fill loop, not silently truncated
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-io.test.ts > fills the buffer across a short read - PASS (10 one-byte reads assembled)

behaviors.behavior: Truncation mid-read re-stats so the caller clamps to the new size
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-io.test.ts > re-stats after a short read - PASS

behaviors.behavior: Truncation below the read start drops bytes rather than relabelling them
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-io.test.ts > drops bytes that the post-truncation size can no longer address - PASS; VERIFIED TO FAIL without the fix

behaviors.behavior: A partial read is capped at the post-truncation end
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-io.test.ts > caps a partial read at the post-truncation end - PASS; VERIFIED TO FAIL without the fix

behaviors.behavior: A re-stat that GREW cannot inflate the clamp
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-io.test.ts > never reports a size larger than the one the read observed - PASS

behaviors.behavior: The file handle is closed when read() throws
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-io.test.ts > closes the handle when the read throws - PASS

behaviors.behavior: The file handle is closed when fstat() throws
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-io.test.ts > closes the handle when fstat throws - PASS

behaviors.behavior: A close() failure never replaces the read error, so retry classification is preserved
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-io.test.ts > propagates the READ error, not a close() failure - PASS

behaviors.behavior: A close() failure never fails an otherwise successful read
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-io.test.ts > does not fail a successful read because close() threw - PASS

behaviors.behavior: Output equivalence with the whole-file reader across 12 input classes
behaviors.evidence: server session-watcher.readchunk-positional.test.ts > 12 parameterised cases against a reference implementation of the old algorithm, plus absolute invariants - ALL PASS (from 0, mid-line, on-boundary, past EOF, negative, no-newline tail, empty, single newline, multi-byte UTF-8 aligned and mid-character)

behaviors.behavior: Rotation is still detected before any read happens
behaviors.evidence: server session-watcher.readchunk-positional.test.ts > still reports rotation before reading anything - PASS

behaviors.behavior: status=missing is still reported when no JSONL exists
behaviors.evidence: server session-watcher.readchunk-positional.test.ts > still reports missing when no JSONL exists - PASS

behaviors.behavior: Sequential polling of a growing file duplicates and skips nothing
behaviors.evidence: server session-watcher.readchunk-positional.test.ts > walks a growing file with no duplicated or skipped bytes - PASS

behaviors.behavior: fromByte is forwarded to the reader rather than discarded
behaviors.evidence: server session-watcher.readchunk-positional.test.ts > AC-1 forwards fromByte to the reader - PASS

behaviors.behavior: A one-shot EBUSY on the read is retried, not surfaced
behaviors.evidence: server session-watcher.readchunk-positional.test.ts > AC-3 a one-shot EBUSY on the tail read is retried - PASS

behaviors.behavior: ENOENT is retryable on the read but fatal for discovery

behaviors.behavior: A short read still yields a newline-terminated chunk with a matching toByte
behaviors.evidence: server session-watcher.readchunk-positional.test.ts > AC-4 a short read still yields a newline-terminated chunk - PASS

behaviors.behavior: Bytes appended mid-read defer to the next poll and stay cursor-safe
behaviors.evidence: server session-watcher.readchunk-positional.test.ts > defers bytes appended mid-read without duplicating or skipping - PASS

behaviors.behavior: readChunk clamps against the size the read observed, not the discovery stat

behaviors.behavior: The readWithRetry envelope is unchanged by the module move
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-retry.test.ts > 4 cases (3 moved verbatim plus the new ENOENT-policy case) - PASS

behaviors.behavior: lastIndexOfByte is unchanged by the module move
behaviors.evidence: server session-jsonl-retry.test.ts > 3 cases - PASS

behaviors.behavior: The other two production callers (inbox derive, transcript route) are unaffected
behaviors.evidence: covered-by-existing-test - full server suite 250 files / 2931 passed and client 311 files / 2912 passed, including the inbox-derive and transcript-route suites

behaviors.behavior: A bounded range is served over real HTTP against a multi-MB transcript, the pane stays live, and resuming from toByte delivers the append exactly once
behaviors.evidence: client e2e/flows/91-transcript-positional-tail.spec.ts - PASS against a real 2 MB seeded JSONL in Chromium; regression family 32 / 37a / 38 / 90 also PASS (5/5 total)

behaviors.behavior: The measured speed-up holds for the SHIPPED module, not just the design
behaviors.evidence: probe importing the real core/session-jsonl-io.ts and SessionWatcher over the 203-transcript corpus: 919 MB / 253 ms became 102 MB / 44 ms; readChunk on the 137.9 MB transcript = 2.22 ms, status ok, 523,621 chars, newline-terminated

behaviors.behavior: Cold-page-cache timing on a 137.9 MB transcript
behaviors.reason: The OS page cache cannot be evicted deterministically from a test, so only the warm figure is measurable. The warm number is the conservative one - a cold read strictly worsens the whole-file column and leaves the positional column roughly flat - so the claim is not weakened by the gap.

surface_verification.notes: Isolated single-process stack: Hono on 127.0.0.1:3848 with SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local, SHIPWRIGHT_STATIC_DIR pointed at a client/dist built fresh in this worktree (the main-tree dist predated the latest client commits), and USERPROFILE/HOME redirected to a temp profile so that ~/.claude/projects and ~/.shipwright-webui were isolated. Ran the new spec 91 plus the transcript regression family 32 / 37a / 38 / 90. 5 passed, 0 failed, on the first attempt. surface=web is owed under the backend-affects-frontend rule: readChunk backs GET /api/external/tasks/:id/transcript, which the task-detail pane polls at 1 Hz, even though no client source file changed.
